title: Historical Next-Compound Transition Priors
summary: Public F1 strategy guides repeatedly frame races as a small set of viable stint templates, not just independent stop/no-stop decisions.
rationale: Public F1 strategy guides repeatedly frame races as a small set of viable stint templates, not just independent stop/no-stop decisions. The current baseline reasons about whether tyres can finish, but not about what tyre choice typically follows a stop in similar contexts.
implementation_hint: From training only, learn past-only empirical priors for the next compound after a stop conditioned on current Compound, laps remaining, wet/dry regime, stint index, and compounds already used. Convert these into features for the current row: probability that a stop now would lead to a historically common next compound and probability that the resulting plan can finish legally.
expected_effect: Can capture one-stop vs two-stop template preferences and compound-switch habits that plain tyre-life windows miss.
risk: Sparse categories may overfit, so use hierarchical smoothing and leave-group-out estimation.
